text:
PET/CT SCAN WITH FDG — WHOLE BODY

EXAMINATION: FDG-PET/CT, skull base through mid-thigh (whole body staging protocol)
INDICATION: 52-year-old female with biopsy-proven HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue, T2N1M0 by prior CT neck. Presenting for whole-body staging prior to initiation of definitive therapy.
COMPARISON: CT neck with contrast obtained recently (reviewed for correlation).

TECHNIQUE:
Patient fasted for greater than 6 hours prior to examination. Serum glucose at time of injection was 88 mg/dL. 10.2 mCi of F-18 FDG was administered intravenously. Standard uptake period of approximately 60 minutes followed prior to image acquisition. Whole body PET images were acquired from skull base to mid-thigh with concurrent low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. No IV contrast administered with the CT portion of this examination. Images were reconstructed using iterative reconstruction algorithm and reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes as well as fused PET/CT images. Maximum intensity projection (MIP) images reviewed.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK — PRIMARY TUMOR SITE:
There is a conspicuous focus of markedly increased FDG uptake in the right base of tongue corresponding to the primary tumor site. The hypermetabolic lesion measures approximately 3.1 cm in its greatest transaxial dimension on co-registered CT, with a peak SUVmax of 12.4. The uptake is heterogeneous, with the most avid component centered at the right posterolateral base of tongue extending toward the right glossoepiglottic fold. The lesion is right-lateralized without convincing metabolic activity crossing the midline of the tongue base. No abnormal FDG uptake is identified to suggest involvement of the vallecula, epiglottis, pharyngeal walls, or soft palate. The airway appears patent without evidence of extrinsic compression.

No abnormal uptake is seen in the oral cavity, hard or soft palate, nasopharynx, or larynx above background.

CERVICAL LYMPH NODES:
A single focus of increased FDG activity is identified at the right level IIA station, corresponding to a lymph node measuring approximately 2.4 cm on CT with an SUVmax of 8.7. Morphologically this node demonstrates internal heterogeneity on the CT component. No additional FDG-avid nodes are identified at the right levels I, IIB, III, IV, or V. No contralateral left-sided hypermetabolic adenopathy. No bilateral retropharyngeal, parapharyngeal, or parotid region abnormal uptake. No supraclavicular or infraclavicular nodal activity.

THORAX:
The lungs are clear. No focal FDG-avid pulmonary nodules or masses. No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy with abnormal FDG uptake. Incidentally noted are a few small subcentimeter pulmonary nodules seen on the CT component (largest approximately 3 mm in the right lower lobe), too small to characterize metabolically, almost certainly benign given size and morphology. No pleural or pericardial effusions. The heart demonstrates expected physiologic myocardial uptake. The thyroid gland is without focal abnormal uptake; diffuse mildly heterogeneous uptake is seen bilaterally, likely physiologic, though clinical correlation with thyroid function tests is suggested given the patient's known history of hypothyroidism.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
The liver demonstrates homogeneous background FDG uptake without focal hypermetabolic lesions. The spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands are without focal abnormal activity. No suspicious periportal or portacaval adenopathy. The kidneys show expected excretory FDG activity bilaterally. No abnormal pelvic lymph node activity. The bowel and bladder demonstrate physiologic uptake. No pelvic masses or suspicious foci.

SKELETON:
No focal hypermetabolic osseous lesions to suggest skeletal metastatic involvement. No lytic or blastic lesions identified on CT correlation. Bone marrow FDG activity is within normal limits.

SOFT TISSUES:
No suspicious soft tissue masses or hypermetabolic foci outside the head and neck region.

IMPRESSION:

1. Intensely FDG-avid right base of tongue primary tumor (SUVmax 12.4, approximately 3.1 cm), consistent with the known HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma. Right-lateralized without metabolic evidence of midline involvement.

2. Single FDG-avid right level IIA cervical lymph node (SUVmax 8.7, 2.4 cm), consistent with nodal metastasis. No additional nodal disease identified above or below the clavicles.

3. No evidence of distant metastatic disease. Specifically, no suspicious FDG-avid foci in the lungs, liver, bones, or elsewhere.

4. Incidental tiny right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, almost certainly benign; no metabolic activity; no follow-up recommended in the context of a staging examination of this kind, though routine follow-up per incidental nodule guidelines is deferred to the treating team's discretion.

5. Overall findings are consistent with locoregional disease confined to the oropharynx and ipsilateral cervical nodal station — staging T2N1M0 by metabolic and anatomic criteria.

Signing Radiologist: [Attending Nuclear Medicine/Radiology Physician]
Dictated and reviewed.
